summary: 0x1407B8460 is the CRT-style int-returning atexit wrapper. It calls 0x1407B8424, then converts a non-null returned function pointer into success code 0 and a null return into failure code -1.

0x1407B8424 is the pointer-returning onexit gate. It preserves the incoming callback in RBX, selects the default dynamic context through 0x1407D9174 while the preinit gate byte at 0x140C55FEF is still -1, otherwise passes 0x140C55FF0 plus the callback into 0x1407D91F0. On successful registration it returns the original callback pointer; on failure it returns null.
0x1407D9174 is a tiny thunk that moves the callback from RCX to RDX, loads context 0x140C56B48 into RCX, and jumps to 0x1407D91F0.
0x1407D91F0 builds the stack-local registration tuple and calls 0x1407D8E2C, which is the locked dynamic on-exit registration core mapped in the prior exit-callback-table milestone.

0x1407B82B0 initializes or resets the process-local onexit table pair. It validates mode ECX <= 1, initializes both triads 0x140C55FF0 and 0x140C56008 from the sentinel vector at 0x140BAD0C0 plus end slot -1, and marks 0x140C55FE8 initialized. In mode 0 it first tries to reset both tables through 0x1407D91C8 when prior state permits.

The two direct callers of 0x1407B82B0 are startup/lifecycle roots: 0x1407B8480 passes mode 1 during broader CRT startup, and 0x1407B86EC passes mode 0 while initializing condition-variable compatibility shims.

The native pe-callsite-registers analyzer recovered 221 direct calls to 0x1407B8460. Every one has a decoded static LEA RCX, [callback] setup, so the full dynamic atexit callback inventory is now machine-readable rather than a raw caller list.

startup_callbacks.summary: Deletes the critical section at 0x140C56030 and closes the fallback event handle at 0x140C56028 if one was created. This pairs with the compatibility path that resolves SleepConditionVariableCS/WakeAllConditionVariable or creates a fallback event.
Walks the qword range 0x140BB22B8..0x140BB22B8 and guard-dispatches non-null entries through 0x140803060. The observed range is empty, so this currently behaves as a reserved terminator-table walker.

tooling_updates.change: Added a native PE analyzer and CLI command that recovers simple static register setup before direct callsites.
tooling_updates.why_it_matters: The dynamic on-exit callback inventory depends on instruction-boundary-safe recovery of RCX callback pointers before calls to 0x1407B8460. A raw byte scan produced false positives when immediate data contained opcode-looking bytes; the new tool uses decoded instruction windows and is reusable for other wrapper/callback sites.

bounded_inference: This milestone maps the CRT/dynamic-onexit layer and a complete static inventory of atexit callback pointers. Most of the 221 callbacks are still unnamed destructors or subsystem shutdown handlers; the next reversing pass should cluster those callback bodies by call/import/literal profile and separate CRT scaffolding from RuneScape client-specific teardown.
next_steps: Run pe-runtime-functions and pe-callsite-registers driven clustering over the 221 callback targets to identify destructor families and real client subsystems.
Map callback bodies around 0x1407F54xx..0x1407F86xx because they dominate the recovered static inventory.
Follow the broader startup root 0x1407B8480 into the non-CRT client bootstrap path after pruning the dynamic-exit scaffolding.
